title: Ganglion cyst
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: A ganglion cyst is a fluid-filled swelling often near a joint or tendon.
Ganglion cysts commonly occur on the wrist or hand.
They can be painless or cause discomfort with movement or pressure.
Infection, neurovascular symptoms, trauma, rapidly enlarging mass, or diagnostic uncertainty require separate evaluation.
